title: Maintaining a Living Project Initiation Document (PID)
summary: Guidelines for regularly updating the Project Initiation Document (PID) to reflect scope changes, emerging risks, and team adjustments, including version control and stakeholder communication.
content: Treat the PID as a living document; update sections when scope changes, new risks emerge, or team composition shifts. Record version changes and re‑circulate to keep all stakeholders aligned.
can_answer: When should I revise the PID?
How can I track changes and versions of the PID?
examples: Version 1.0 – initial sign‑off; Version 1.1 – added new risk of data privacy regulation.
warnings: Neglecting updates can cause the PID to become outdated and lose credibility.
